description:
**L’azienda giusta per te!**

**MadeInBeauty** è un’azienda specializzata nel mondo dell’estetica avanzata e del benessere. Siamo presenti sul territorio nazionale in **7 città** e pronti a portare il nostro progetto nelle principali città d’Italia.

Ma lasciamo parlare i numeri dello scorso anno:

* **26\.500 clienti gestiti**
* **46\.600 appuntamenti svolti**
* **4\.800 nuovi clienti acquisiti**

Traguardi raggiunti grazie all’impegno, alla professionalità e alla dedizione delle nostre collaboratrici e dei nostri collaboratori.

Il nostro segreto?  
Aver individuato figure di valore, con una forte predisposizione all’attenzione verso il cliente, supportate da percorsi formativi tenuti da **veri professionisti** direttamente nei nostri centri. Oggi anche tu puoi entrare a far parte del nostro team e crescere professionalmente insieme a noi.

Chi stiamo cercando

Per la nostra unità produttiva di **Bergamo**, siamo alla ricerca di **un/una Estetista qualificato/a** da inserire in un contesto dinamico e operativo, dedicato all’erogazione di trattamenti estetici avanzati attraverso l’utilizzo di tecnologie di ultima generazione.

Per MadeInBeauty è fondamentale incontrare persone attente al dettaglio, capaci di ascoltare il cliente e di prendersi cura delle sue esigenze con professionalità, sensibilità e competenza.

Posizione

La figura selezionata si occuperà della **customer experience** e delle seguenti attività:

* consulenza estetica professionale;
* vendita di prodotti e servizi;
* esecuzione di trattamenti laser, viso e corpo;
* utilizzo di macchinari estetici e tecnologie di ultima generazione.

Requisiti

Cerchiamo una persona con:

* ottima attitudine commerciale e orientamento alla vendita;
* diploma di estetista / attestato in estetica;
* competenza, professionalità e cura del dettaglio;
* capacità organizzativa e autonomia operativa;
* ottima dialettica, ordine e precisione;
* predisposizione al lavoro in team e al raggiungimento degli obiettivi.

Cosa offriamo

Offriamo inserimento diretto secondo **CCNL Acconciatura ed Estetica**, in una realtà solida, in forte crescita e orientata all’innovazione.

**Diversamente da quanto inizialmente previsto, la fase contrattuale iniziale prevederà un’assunzione part\-time di 24 ore settimanali**, con una **retribuzione proporzionata** a quanto già indicato nell’annuncio.  
Questa prima fase sarà finalizzata alla conoscenza e alla valutazione della risorsa, con l’obiettivo di procedere successivamente alla **trasformazione all’orario ordinario previsto dagli standard MadeInBeauty**, pari a **36 o 40 ore settimanali**, distribuite su 6 giorni lavorativi.

MadeInBeauty offre un inquadramento a norma di legge, supportato da un sistema di incentivi economici di sicuro interesse. La nostra filosofia si ispira al raggiungimento di risultati di qualità in ogni fase del lavoro, attraverso modelli innovativi, strutturati ed efficaci.

Per questo desideriamo entrare in contatto con persone dinamiche, flessibili, rigorose e motivate, capaci di contribuire attivamente al lavoro quotidiano, appassionarsi alla cura del dettaglio e operare nel rispetto di elevati standard qualitativi e procedurali.

title: IIoT Engineer
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**Company Introduction**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer.


We support our global manufacturing sites by developing digital and IIoT solutions that enhance operational excellence, data\-driven decision\-making, and smart manufacturing capabilities across EU and APAC regions.

**DESCRIPTION**


Join the PPI (Practical Process Improvement) Digital Transformation team at Thermo Fisher Scientific as an **IIoT Engineer**, where you will contribute to the design, implementation, and support of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) solutions across multiple GMP\-regulated manufacturing sites.


You will be part of a **structured IIoT sub\-team**, working alongside with a Senior IIoT Engineer, and collaborating closely with Automation, IT, Data Engineering, Data Science, and Digital Project Management teams.


The role focuses on enabling reliable, secure, and scalable data acquisition from shop floor equipment to digital platforms, bridging OT and IT environments. Success in this position requires a hands\-on mindset, strong collaboration skills, and the ability to operate effectively in a multi\-site, matrix organization.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Conduct technical AS\-IS assessments of production lines through document analysis, shop floor walk\-throughs, and interviews with Operations, Maintenance, and Automation stakeholders.
* Design TO\-BE IIoT connectivity architectures using ISA\-95 principles and system modeling techniques.
* Configure, test, and support industrial gateways and edge solutions (e.g. Kepware, Ignition) for PLC integration and edge\-to\-cloud data pipelines (OPC UA, MQTT, Modbus TCP).
* Integrate PLCs and automation systems with IIoT platforms, ensuring data consistency, reliability, and performance.
* Test and troubleshoot OT networks and data pipelines, validating data quality, throughput, and stability.
* Collaborate with Data Engineering teams to ensure seamless ingestion of OT data into data lakes and analytics platforms.
* Participate in technical discussions and reviews with vendors, system integrators, and internal stakeholders, contributing alternative solutions and improvement proposals.
* Support validation and compliance activities by producing technical documentation (architecture diagrams, data flow models, configuration documentation, test evidence).
* Apply industrial cybersecurity and networking principles to ensure secure and reliable OT/IT integration.
* Contribute to digital transformation initiatives supporting smart manufacturing, predictive maintenance, OEE monitoring, and performance analytics.

**Site \& Collaboration Model**


The IIoT Engineer is expected to work effectively within a **distributed team**, collaborating remotely with colleagues across different manufacturing sites within EU/APAC sites.

**REQUIREMENTS**

**Minimum Qualifications**

* Technical diploma with **3–5 years of hands\-on experience** in OT/IT environments, **or**
* Bachelor’s degree in Automation Engineering, Electronics, Computer Engineering, or related STEM disciplines with relevant industrial experience.
* At least **2 years of experience** in automation, IIoT implementation, or system integration within manufacturing or process industries.
* Hands\-on experience with industrial communication protocols (OPC UA, MQTT, Modbus TCP).
* Experience configuring industrial gateways or edge platforms (e.g. Kepware, Ignition).
* Experience integrating PLCs (Siemens S7 preferred; B\&R or Rockwell acceptable).
* Working knowledge of IT/OT networking concepts (VLANs, subnetting, firewalls, DMZ).
* Familiarity with ISA\-95 models and system architecture diagramming tools.
* Basic knowledge of SQL databases and scripting for automation or data handling.
* Fluency in Italian and English (minimum B1 level for technical communication).

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Certifications in IIoT platforms or industrial communication standards.
* Experience in GMP/FDA\-regulated manufacturing environments and exposure to Computerized System Validation (CSV).
* Experience with SCADA systems (Ignition, GE iFix, Wonderware).
* Familiarity with cloud IoT services (Azure IoT Hub, AWS IoT Core).
* Exposure to data ingestion, streaming architectures, or event\-driven systems.
* Experience in digital manufacturing, pharmaceutical, or multi\-site environments.
* Background in system integrators, OEMs, or industrial solution providers.

**Key Competencies \& Soft Skills**

* Structured problem\-solving mindset with the ability to analyze complex OT/IT systems.
* Strong collaboration skills across Automation, IT, Operations, Data Engineering, and Project Management teams.
* Openness to technical guidance, peer review, and standardized ways of working within the IIoT domain.
* Hands\-on and pragmatic approach with strong troubleshooting capabilities.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities and execute tasks under technical leadership.
* Attention to detail in technical documentation and configuration activities.
* Effective communication skills, including the ability to explain technical topics to non\-technical stakeholders.
* Adaptability to work across multiple sites, regions, and cultural contexts (EU/APAC).
* Strong commitment to reliability, safety, and quality in GMP\-regulated environments.

**Additional Information**

* Occasional travel may be required to support site activities and collaboration (**estimated 0–25%**).

description:
**Work Schedule**


Rotational shift nights/weekends**Environmental Conditions**


Cleanroom: no hair products, jewelry, makeup, nail polish, perfume, exposed piercings, facial hair etc... allowed, Cold Room/Freezers \-22degreesF/\-6degrees C, Fluctuating Temps hot/cold, Loud Noises (Equipment/Machinery), Strong Odors (chemical, lubricants, biological products etc.), Various outside weather conditions, Warehouse, Will work with hazardous/toxic materials**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.

**Location/Division Specific Information:**  

The position operates within the site’s technical/maintenance department, ensuring the proper functioning of systems supporting production, in compliance with company SOPs, GMP regulations, and safety standards. The role involves continuous interaction with internal departments and external technical service providers.

**How Will You Make an Impact?:**  

You will contribute by ensuring the efficiency and reliability of systems through preventive, predictive, and corrective maintenance activities, responding promptly to faults even during production phases. You will also support continuous improvement by proposing definitive solutions to recurring issues and optimizing equipment downtime and restart times.

**A Day in the Life:**  

Example 1: Start\-up and verification of facility systems, monitoring consumables, and managing scheduled maintenance activities through the maintenance management system.  

Example 2: Troubleshooting a system failure during production, coordinating with the Team Leader, and collaborating with external technicians to quickly restore operations.  

Example 3: Analyzing recurring issues, proposing improvement solutions, and implementing actions to reduce equipment downtime.

  

Education: (NA or APAC)  

High school diploma or equivalent required.  

Education: (EMEA)  

High School Diploma, Secondary Education, Advanced Certificate, or equivalent. (\*\*\*If a Degree or specific Apprenticeship or Technical Certification is required please modify the first sentence and add the specifics here\*\*\*)  

  

Experience:  

Required: Minimum of 2 years of work experience in a manufacturing, operations, production, lab setting or related field  

Preferred: Experience in a cGMP environment  

Preferred: Experience working in a clean room environment (Remove if not relevant)  

Preferred: Experience testing complex electromechanical systems (Remove if not relevant)

**Knowledge, Skills, Abilities:**  

Example 1: Strong knowledge of GMP and safety regulations, with the ability to operate in compliance with company SOPs and use maintenance management and IT systems effectively.  

Example 2: Solid technical background, including the ability to read pneumatic diagrams (and electrical diagrams as a plus), with knowledge of thermodynamics, water treatment, freeze\-drying, and sterilization processes.  

Example 3: High level of precision and organizational skills, ability to manage multiple activities simultaneously, collaborate with internal teams and external service providers, and proactively propose solutions to recurring technical issues.

**Physical Requirements / Work Environment**  

The role is based in a production/manufacturing environment and involves working directly on facility systems and equipment. It may require operating during established company working hours, intervening on systems during production phases, and coordinating on\-site activities with external technical personnel, in full compliance with safety regulations.

**What We Offer:**  

The opportunity to work in a structured and regulated environment, contributing directly to production reliability and continuous improvement initiatives. The role provides cross\-functional exposure, collaboration with internal departments and external partners, and professional growth within the technical/maintenance field.

title: Customer Quality Specialist
description:
**Who we are:** IRCA Group, an Advent portfolio company, was founded in Italy in 1919, and it is a leading international B2B provider of high\-quality semi\-finished ingredients such as chocolate, decorations, creams, inclusions, nuts and pistachios, pastry mixes, gelato ingredients, and other essential solutions for industrial clients or other independent artisanal producers.



With more than 2\.400 employees, 22 production facilities worldwide, and €1\.3 billion in revenues, IRCA Group has a solid track record of growth and international expansion.



**What do we offer:**

* An international and inclusive environment, with opportunities to work on global projects and collaborate with colleagues worldwide
* Career growth opportunities, including possibilities abroad
* Meal vouchers (€8 for each day spent in the office)
* Health insurance coverage as per national labor contract
* Free company parking
* Company laptop
### **The role:**


We are seeking a Customer Quality Specialist to join the plant Quality function, supporting the Plant Quality Manager of the production site in the implementation and maintenance of the Quality and Food Safety Management System. The role will focus on customer complaint management and non‑conformity oversight, ensuring full compliance of products, processes, and raw materials with applicable regulations, internal standards, and certification requirements### **What will you do:**


* Manage **customer complaints end****‑****to****‑****end**, including intake, timely first follow‑up within the working day, and ongoing coordination until closure
* Perform **root cause analysis** and define corrective and preventive actions (CAPA), coordinating cross‑functional teams when required to eliminate identified issues
* Oversee **non****‑****conformity management**, ensuring effective implementation, documentation, and follow‑up of corrective and preventive actions
* Prepare clear and structured internal reports and **customer\-facing reports** related to complaints, investigations, and resolutions
* Monitor, analyze, and regularly update **customer complaint KPIs** to support performance tracking and continuous improvement
* Act as the primary point of contact for key customers, ensuring effective and professional communication on quality matters
* Manage **customer communications** in case of product recall, including email exchanges, contact retrieval, and continuous update of involved customer records
* Support documentation management and ensure full traceability of customer communications, especially in critical quality situations
* Support the management and maintenance of the Quality and Food Safety Management System, including HACCP and GMP requirements
* Contribute to product traceability and recall readiness activities in line with food safety regulations and internal standards
* Collaborate on continuous improvement initiatives aimed at strengthening the effectiveness and robustness of the Quality and Food Safety system
### **Requirements:**


* **Education:** Degree in Food Science \& Technology or related fields.
* **Professional Skills:** Knowledge of HACCP principles; understanding of food quality and safety requirements within food manufacturing environments; ability to manage quality\-related activities and customer interactions
* **Experience:** At least 1\+ years of experience in a similar role
* **Language Skills:** Proficiency in Italian and English is mandatory. Additional language skills are a plus
* **Computer Skills:** Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
* **Behavioural Requirements:** Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills; team\-oriented mindset with a proactive approach to customer relationship management; high level of accuracy, autonomy, and result orientation
IRCA fosters a dynamic environment, valuing different perspectives and international experiences, offering a stimulating context. We promote professional growth and career opportunities locally and internationally. With locations worldwide, we offer the chance to work on international projects, collaborate across cultures, and gain a global perspective. If you are looking for stimulating challenges and the chance to contribute your ideas for process improvement in an ever\-growing company, IRCA is the right place.  


*We welcome applications from candidates of all genders, backgrounds, identities, nationality, ethnic origin, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, parental responsibilities, age, religion, or belief. We believe in equal opportunities for all and ensure that our hiring process complies with the highest standards of non\-discrimination and inclusivity. IRCA Group is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment where everyone can be their authentic selves, reach their full potential, and feel a true sense of belonging. Join us in creating a workplace where diversity is valued, and everyone can thrive.*  


*Il presente annuncio è rivolto ai candidati appartenenti a qualsiasi orientamento o espressione di genere, nel rispetto dei principi di diversità e inclusione e di non discriminazione ai sensi del D.lgs. 198/2006\.*

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. description:

Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life \- enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while taking science a step beyond through research, development and delivery of life\-changing therapies. With clinical trials conducted in 100\+ countries and ongoing development of novel frameworks for clinical research through our PPD clinical research portfolio, our work spans laboratory, digital and decentralized clinical trial services. Your determination to deliver quality and accuracy will improve health outcomes that people and communities depend on – now and in the future.

**Discover Impactful Work:**


The (Associate) Medical Director will provide medical support and medical advice for clinical trials and safety evaluation within the PPD Pharmacovigilance department.

**A day in the Life:**

* Provide medical leadership to the study teams, including creating clinical trial protocols while implementing the latest scientific information, contribute to site selection, involved in review and approval of study protocols and amendments, Clinical Study Reports (CSRs), Informed Consent Forms (ICFs), Case Report Forms (CRFs), protocol deviation decisions and other clinical documents as required for the conduct of clinical trials
* Real time Medical Monitoring of clinical studies: ensure medical validity of each individual primary endpoint as well study subject safety, while ensuring medical issues are identified early. Pro\-active customer concern and correction of issues in consultation with the Global Medical Indication Lead
* During initial review of protocol, consults with internal groups and support staff regarding procedural and budgetary items and the necessity for change due to any subsequent protocol amendments. Acquires other functions’ dedication to close gaps and address issues timely and effectively. Serves as chief liaison between sponsor and all PPD internal departments performing the required tasks during all study phases. Educates Investigator sites by delivering a protocol specific lab procedures presentation and demonstration during an investigator meeting. Controls, handles and follows day to day activities during the course of the clinical trial to resolve any issues and answer queries
* Medical point of contact for all internal and external stakeholders: Interact with the investigators as needed, answer questions of IRBs and Health Authorities, prepare and present material to the study executive and independent safety committee if applicable; medical training of site staff at Investigator meetings; CRA training in new indications
* Ensure study compliance for all medical aspects by understanding and applying all relevant SOPs and GCP
* Closely collaborate with the cross functional study team members in order for medical risks, issues, and results to be clear to all involved parties

**Education**

* Medical Doctor, with a specialty in Rheumatology, shown clinical experience treating patients in hospital.

**Knowledge, Skills, Abilities**

* Significant experience within pharmaceutical industry, clinical trials and pharmaceutical medicine is critically important
* Is fluent in spoken and written English
* Shown understanding and experience with NDA submission process.
* Shown understanding of regulatory guidelines for adverse event reporting
* Strong communication \& presentation skills and is a strong teammate


Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Watch as our colleagues explain 5 reasons to work with us. As one team of 100,000\+ colleagues, we share a common set of values \- Integrity, Intensity, Innovation and Involvement \- working together to accelerate research, solve complex scientific challenges, drive technological innovation and support patients in need. \#StartYourStory with PPD, part of Thermo Fisher Scientific, where diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives are valued.

title: Senior Global Compensation Analyst
description:
**At Cision, we believe in empowering every individual to make an impact. Here, your voice is heard, your ideas are valued, and your unique perspective fuels our collective success. As part of our global team, you'll thrive in an environment that champions curiosity, collaboration, and innovation, all while making meaningful contributions to the brands we accelerate.**

  


**Join us in shaping the future of communication and building authentic connections that matter. Whether you're solving complex problems or driving bold innovations, your growth is our success, and together, we'll create the conversations of tomorrow.**

  


**Empower your impact at Cision. Be seen, be understood, be you.**

  



This role plays a key part in the administration, analysis, and execution of global compensation programs, including salary structures, incentive plans, and annual compensation cycles. The position provides compensation analysis, job architecture governance, and support for compensation planning activities to ensure pay practices remain competitive, equitable, and aligned with the organisation's compensation philosophy and frameworks.

  


**What you'll do:**

  


* Support the execution and administration of global compensation programs and annual compensation cycles, including merit planning, promotions, bonus planning, and incentive processes.
* Conduct compensation analysis such as pay equity reviews, market benchmarking, compa\-ratio analysis, and compensation modelling to support informed pay decisions.
* Review and validate compensation recommendations to ensure alignment with compensation philosophy, salary structures, internal equity, and budget guidelines.
* Participate in external compensation surveys, including job matching, data validation, submission, and interpretation of market results to help shape compensation strategies.
* Support the development, maintenance, and governance of job architecture, including job structures, grades, levelling, and compensation frameworks.
* Prepare compensation reports, dashboards, modelling, and analysis to support annual compensation planning, bonus funding, workforce planning, and leadership decision\-making.
* Maintain compensation data integrity and ensure compliance with compensation policies, guidelines, and relevant regulatory requirements.
* Identify trends, anomalies, and areas requiring further review or escalation.
* Partner with People Partners, Talent Acquisition, Finance, and business leaders to provide consultative compensation guidance and analysis.
* Perform other duties and responsibilities as required to support business needs.

  


**What you'll bring:**

  


* Typical experience: 5\+ years in a related compensation, rewards, or analytical role.
* Actual experience may vary depending on role complexity, geography, internal development opportunities, or a comparable mix of training, education, and experience.
* Experience working with compensation data, salary structures, incentive plans, and compensation planning processes.
* Strong spreadsheet and data analysis skills, including advanced spreadsheet functionality.
* Experience supporting compensation cycles such as merit increases, promotions, or bonus planning.
* Familiarity with compensation benchmarking and market data analysis.
* Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and data interpretation skills.
* Ability to manage sensitive and confidential information with discretion.
* Ability to work effectively in a collaborative, cross\-functional environment.

  


**Preferred qualifications:**

  


* University degree preferred.
* Experience supporting global or multi\-country compensation programs.
* Experience participating in compensation survey submissions and job matching (e.g. Radford, Mercer, Willis Towers Watson).
* Experience working with job architecture, salary grades, and compensation frameworks.
* Experience using HRIS and compensation planning tools such as UKG, Workday, SuccessFactors, or similar systems.
* Professional certification in compensation or human resources (e.g. CCP, GRP, PHR, or equivalent).
* Experience working in a fast\-paced, data\-driven, or global organisation.

  


**What we offer:**

  



Upon joining Cision, you will have access to a comprehensive benefits package, fully or partially funded by the company, including:


* Health Insurance
* Food Vouchers
* Flexible Benefits Allowance
* Employee Assistance Programme (EAP)
* Cision Global Charitable Giving Program
* Headspace

  


**If this opportunity feels like the right fit for you, we'd love to hear from you!** Please note that for this role, we are only able to consider candidates who are already based in the country of employment.

  


**\#LI\-FR1 \#Remote**

  



As a global leader in PR, marketing and social media management technology and intelligence, Cision helps brands and organizations to identify, connect and engage with customers and stakeholders to drive business results. PR Newswire, a network of over 1\.1 billion influencers, in\-depth monitoring, analytics and its Brandwatch and Falcon.io social media platforms headline a premier suite of solutions. Cision has offices in 24 countries throughout the Americas, EMEA and APAC. For more information about Cision's award\-winning solutions, including its next\-gen Cision Communications Cloud®, visit www.cision.com and follow @Cision on Twitter.

title: Young Researcher in Molecular Cell Biology
description:
IRCCS San Raffaele Scientific Institute in Milan–Italy, is looking for a young motivated researcher to join the Cell Adhesion Laboratory directed by Prof. Ivan de Curtis. The candidate will work on a project aimed at the analysis of molecular\-cellular mechanisms implicated in tumor cell invasion, to identify new targets to interfere with the formation of metastases. The project financed by AIRC is dealing with the functional characterization of a molecular network that regulates the protrusive activity of invasive tumor cells (de Curtis I, 2021, Trends Cell Biol, doi: 10\.1016/j.tcb.2020\.12\.002; Pehkonen H, de Curtis I, 2021, Oncogene doi: 10\.1038/s41388\-021\-02048\-1\). This protein network includes the scaffold proteins Liprin\-a1 and ERC1 (Ribolla, de Curtis I, 2025, Commun Biol, doi: 10\.1038/s42003\-025\-08470\-5\) and regulatory enzymes (Ramella, de Curtis, 2024, iScience doi: 10\.1016/j.isci.2024\.109440; Ripamonti M, de Curtis I, 2022, Commun Biol doi: 10\.1038/s42003\-022\-03989\-3\) undergoing liquid\-liquid phase separation to regulate the invasive properties of breast cancer cells, and to promote the turnover of adhesions and invasion. The candidate will contribute by investigating mechanisms linking Liprin\-a1 and ERC1 proteins to the organization and function of the migratory activity of cancer cells. For this, the candidate will learn and apply cloning to prepare wt/mutant constructs, high resolution confocal microscopy on fixed and live cells, biochemical/molecular cell biology approaches including transfections with siRNAs and plasmids for the KO (CRISPR/Cas9\) of endogenous proteins, functional assays to assess effects on tumor cell invasion.
  
  

Applicants are requested to send an email to decurtis.ivan@hsr.it including:
  
a CV with name(s) and contact(s) (email, phone) of at least one Referee; a brief description of known techniques and previous research experience;
  
a motivation letter describing the interest in joining this lab, and previous experimental experiences. Both postdoctoral candidates and young post\-master degree candidates will be considered, pending their strong motivation in learning approaches applied to important aspects of cancer cell biology.
  
  

**Main activities:**  

See text above.
  
  

**Who we are looking for:**  

A motivated young researcher enthusiastic about learning molecular\-cell biology to address important mechanisms underlying tumor cell invasion.
  
  

**Work details:**  

**Type of contract:** Fellowship/CoCoCo;
  
**Duration:** 12 months renewable;
  
**Place of work:** San Raffaele Scientific Institute, Dibit, via Olgettina 58, 20132 Milano.
  
  

At San Raffaele Scientific Institute, a world\-renowned center of excellence for clinical and scientific activities, we believe that considering diversity and promoting an open and inclusive environment is crucial to our success. We are committed to ensure that equal employment opportunities are provided and based on merit without regard to age, religion, sex, disability, medical condition, sexual orientation, cultural and social backgrounds, gender identity or expression, national or ethnic origin, or any other legally recognized status entitled to protection under applicable laws. We offer attractive conditions and rewards appropriate to an internationally competitive research organization.

title: Biologo per area ricerca
description:
**Posizione:** Biologo per Area Ricerca  
**Orario:** 36 ore settimanali  
**Contratto:** Tempo pieno, in libera professione  
**Sede di lavoro:** Centro Clinico Nemo Milano, Ospedale Niguarda

**Descrizione del ruolo:**  
Il Centro Clinico Nemo, struttura di riferimento nel trattamento di malattie neuromuscolari, ricerca un/a Clinical Study Coordinator: Biologo

**Ruolo e Responsabilità**  
È responsabilità del CSC, in collaborazione con il Direttore Medico Scientifico e con lo Sperimentatore Principale di ciascun progetto di ricerca, verificare che questo venga condotto in accordo alla normativa vigente, alle ICH GCP e secondo quanto indicato nel Protocollo.

La figura del CSC che è collocata all’interno delle Clinical Operations (o ClinOps) del Centro Sperimentale, per lo svolgimento del proprio ruolo, deve soddisfare i requisiti di seguito elencati:

**Requisiti richiesti**:

− laurea in biologia

− GCP Training.

− Training protocollo specifici.

− Training patologia specifici.

**Principali mansioni**:

Il CSC SP è delegato dal Direttore Medico Scientifico e dallo Sperimentatore Principale di ciascuna sperimentazione per il coordinamento della stessa e per l’organizzazione delle attività operative instaurando e mantenendo rapporti costanti con il paziente, con lo Sponsor/CRO e con tutte le altre figure professionali coinvolte nella sperimentazione. In accordo e sotto la responsabilità del PI garantisce, attraverso strategie decisionali, le tempistiche di arruolamento dei pazienti secondo i criteri di inclusione ed esclusione previsti dallo specifico protocollo di ricerca. Inoltre, pianifica gli accessi e le valutazioni per tutti i pazienti coinvolti confrontandosi costantemente con lo staff sperimentazione specifico.

Nello specifico, di seguito vengono elencate le attività delegate al CSC SP per ciascuna sperimentazione:

− supporta e collabora con il team di Regulatory \& Start\-up per organizzare e finalizzare le attività di avvio della sperimentazione;

* partecipa agli incontri organizzati dallo Sponsor/CRO prima dell’avvio della sperimentazione e durante il suo corso;
* interagisce con Sponsor/CRO delegate per la gestione di tutta la documentazione sperimentazione specifica, occupandosi della gestione dell’ISF e/o TMF;
* garantisce l’archiviazione di tutta la documentazione
* fornisce documentazione e reportistica accurata e tempestiva allo Sponsor/CRO,
* identifica e seleziona i pazienti da arruolare in supporto al PI e/o al Sub\-Investigator, documentando la storia medica recente e remota per determinare la conformità ai Criteri di Inclusione previsti dal Protocollo sperimentale;
* somministra i questionari/diari ai pazienti e/o ai genitori;
* prepara i kit per i prelievi centralizzati e il resto dell’occorrente tra elettromedicali/strumenti/materiale necessario per la conduzione della visita;
* se adeguatamente formato, gestisce e manipola i campioni biologici diretti al laboratorio locale e/o centralizzato;
* gestisce e utilizza i sistemi elettronici per l’assegnazione del trattamento sperimentale (IRT/IWRS);
* si coordina e collabora con lo staff medico e non di altre Unità Operative/Strutture coinvolto nelle attività previste dal protocollo sperimentale;
* compila e mantiene aggiornata la modulistica del Sistema Qualità dell’Area Ricerca applicabile a ciascuna sperimentazione per garantirne il monitoraggio di ciascun processo.

**Cosa offriamo**:

* L'opportunità di lavorare in un centro di eccellenza nel campo della neurologia e delle malattie rare.
* Un ambiente di lavoro stimolante, con possibilità di crescita professionale e aggiornamenti continui.
* Opportunità di contribuire in modo significativo a progetti scientifici che migliorano la vita dei pazienti.

**Come candidarsi**  
Se sei appassionato/a di ricerca e desideri contribuire all’innovazione nel campo della salute, invia il tuo CV aggiornato rispondendo a questo annuncio.

Contratto di lavoro: Tempo pieno, Tempo determinato, Contratto con partita IVA

Istruzione:

* Laurea triennale (Obbligatorio)

Abilitazione/Certificazione:

* Iscrizione ordine biologi (Obbligatorio)

title: Research Associate III - Patient Preference and Experience
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**Research Associate III \- Patient Preference and Experience**


This position can be home based across the EMEA region


Thermo Fisher Scientific’s Patient\-Centered Research (PCR) group supports sponsors in incorporating the patient voice in all relevant healthcare decisions, from development over regulatory to reimbursement and product launch. The PCR group blends a unique combination of scientific fields, including health sciences, psychometrics, epidemiology, psychology, health economics and others. Our work has helped identify and clarify unmet clinical needs, assess burden of illness and evaluate patient outcomes, preference and/or adherence, while supporting promotional and labeling claims on a global scale. The advertised role will contribute to this research agenda and help ensure the patient voice is heard.


The Research Associate III will support the delivery of both patient preference and patient experience research by leading scientific input from junior Research Associates and aligning with other functions (e.g., Project Management Office, Editorial Service, Data Collection Management \& Analysis), in close collaboration with study Principal Investigators (PIs). The Research Associate III will adopt an essential role in planning, conducting, reporting and disseminating best\-practice research. This is a client\-facing role, and the successful candidate will have the opportunity to collaborate closely with a wide range of study sponsors across multiple applications. This fast\-pathed and diverse role offers a wide range of opportunities to grow scientific knowledge and consulting skills.

**Es****s****e****n****t****ial F****u****n****c****t****i****o****ns**


Projects


Support in the development and fielding of *both* patient preference *and* experience studies using relevant methodologies (qualitative and quantitative methods including but not limited to online surveys, DCE, BWS, thresholding etc.), including the following specific tasks:

* Support study PIs in the development of study design (including preference instrument experimental design), budget and proposals
* Develop deliverables ready for PI and client review (e.g., protocol, statistical analysis plan, technical report)
* Coordinate and support junior staff throughout projects
* Communicate and manage internal and external recruitment partners (e.g., vendors, patient advocacy groups, other departments)
* Manage data analysis and data quality control
* Contribute to scientific dissemination, where applicable (incl., manuscripts, posters, oral presentations at conferences)
* Report project time accurately at the end of each week
* Contribute to research projects of the wider Patient\-Centered Research (PCR) team, depending on capacity and availability


Teamwork and Quality

* Complete relevant training courses on time (e.g., adverse event training, SOPs, ethics)
* Efficiently delegate and communicate with project teams
* Take active responsibility for on\-the\-job learning of junior project team members
* Work with the Project Management Office to track and drive the project timelines and budget use
* Be familiar with electronic and paper filing requirements, relevant SOPs and IRB needs
* Communicate effectively with clients


Organizational Contributions

* Stay up to date with relevant research
* Contribute to initiatives in the Patient\-Centered Research (PCR) team (e.g., budget\-review committee, practical process improvements, contribution to training in the PCR University, serve as mentor or buddy to junior staff)
* Take part in team meetings and organizational discussions
* May supervise junior staff

**Qualifi****c****a****t****i****o****n****s:**


Education and Experience:

* PhD in relevant scientific discipline ***or***
* Master’s degree in a relevant scientific discipline and at least three years of experience in relevant field ***or***
* Bachelor’s degree in a relevant scientific discipline and at least five years of experience in relevant field


Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:

* Experience and skill in design, implementation, and interpretation of surveys to assess patient experience and preference studies or preference elicitation techniques, including DCEs, BWS and thresholding techniques is essential
* Experience and skill in design, implementation, analysis and interpretation of qualitative research to support development of quantitative surveys and/or preference instruments is essential is required
* Experience in the analysis of quantitative survey data desirable
* Proficient in the use of MS Word, PowerPoint, and Excel
* Experience with PubMed, or similar literature databases, advantageous.
* Outstanding analytical and problem\-solving skills
* Excellent communication and scientific writing skills
* Demonstrated ability to proactively and independently drive research projects


Why Join Us?  

  

When you join Thermo Fisher Scientific, you become part of a global team that values passion, innovation, and a commitment to scientific excellence. You’ll work in an environment where collaboration and development are part of the everyday experience—and where your contributions truly make a difference.  

  

Apply today to help us deliver tomorrow’s breakthroughs.

title: Customs Responsible Italy (f/m/d)
description:

Join us as the Customs Country Responsible for Italy, where you will be the go\-to customs expert and primary contact for all customs\-related matters. You will take charge of organizing and continuously enhancing the customs department, ensuring alignment with the Global Customs Internal Control Program and compliance with Italian customs and trade regulations. Imagine leading a team that drives efficiency and compliance while navigating the complexities of customs legislation and trade compliance. Your role will be pivotal in shaping the future of our customs operations and supporting our mission for sustainable business practices.


**How You’ll Make an Impact**

* You will implement and continuously monitor the Siemens Energy Customs Internal Control Program (ICP) to ensure compliance and efficiency.
* Stay ahead of the curve by monitoring new customs legislation, regulatory changes, and emerging trends that impact our business.
* Develop, implement, and maintain sustainable customs processes that align with our organizational goals.
* Act as the key communication channel between the customs function and Siemens Energy businesses, ensuring visibility on new strategies and organizational changes.
* Provide expert guidance on trade compliance topics for national and regional projects, while coordinating with external business partners like customs brokers.

**What You Bring**

* You hold a complete university degree in Business Administration, Law, or International Trade.
* You have at least 5 years of experience in the field of customs, showcasing your expertise.
* International experience, whether through working in international teams or abroad, is a valuable asset.
* Proficiency in customs/ERP IT systems such as SAP or MIC is essential.
* You are fluent in both Italian and English, with strong organizational capabilities and assertive communication skills.

**About the Team**


You will be part of a dynamic team that thrives in an exciting environment, tackling challenging projects that make a real difference. Our team is dedicated to professional development, offering strong support and collaboration with colleagues from around the globe. You will enjoy a growing function that fosters a dynamic, informal, and inspiring working atmosphere, where your contributions will carry significant responsibility and impact.


**Who is Siemens Energy?**


At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With \+100,000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world’s electricity generation.



Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150\-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.

Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity, we generate power. We run on inclusion, and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character—no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.

* The professional and personal development of our employees is very important to us. With us, you have the opportunity to learn and develop in a self\-determined way. Various attractive programs and measures are available for this purpose.

title: Metering Analyst
description:
**Chi è ENGIE?**



Chi è ENGIE: ENGIE mette al centro delle sue attività (elettricità, gas naturale, efficientamento energetico e servizi) lo sviluppo sostenibile, per far fronte alle sfide della transizione energetica verso una low carbon economy: accesso ad un'economia sostenibile, mitigazione e adattamento al cambiamento climatico e ottimizzazione delle risorse.


**Chi cerchiamo?**



Una risorsa da inserire in presso la nostra sede di **Milano (Via Chiese 72\)**, nell’ambito della Global Business Unit **Supply \& Energy Management** \- **One BTC,** , da inserire all’interno della divisione Customer Operations \& Credit Management con il ruolo di **Metering Analyst.**


*ENGIE One B2C è un operatore energetico leader per il mercato residenziale in sei Paesi, con oltre 20 milioni di contratti attivi. Parte della Global Business Unit Supply \& Energy Management, si concentra su energie rinnovabili e servizi energetici per clienti business e consumer. L’obiettivo è aumentare del 60% la distribuzione di energia elettrica e triplicare la quota di energia verde venduta entro il 2030, promuovendo soluzioni innovative e sostenibili.*


**Che cosa farai?**



La risorsa sarà responsabile della gestione end\-to\-end dei processi legati alle misure per le **commodity power e gas**, assicurando la corretta acquisizione, validazione e messa a disposizione dei dati di consumo a supporto dei processi di fatturazione e credit management.



Il ruolo prevede un presidio costante delle performance operative, con forte orientamento al raggiungimento dei KPI di processo e al rispetto delle scadenze regolatorie e operative.



Particolare rilevanza assume il presidio della normativa di settore e delle disposizioni dell’Autorità di Regolazione per Energia Reti e Ambiente (ARERA), con attenzione a:


* corretta gestione dei flussi di misura nel rispetto delle tempistiche regolatorie;
* monitoraggio delle performance connesse agli obblighi di qualità commerciale e tecnica;
* collaborazione con le funzioni Regulatory e Compliance per garantire l’allineamento continuo all’evoluzione normativa.

  


**Quali saranno le tue responsabilità?**


* Monitorare e governare i flussi di misura power e gas, analizzando e risolvendo in modo strutturato le principali cause di scarto.
* Garantire la tempestiva gestione delle anomalie nel rispetto delle deadline di business e delle scadenze regolatorie.
* Supportare Billing nella gestione di casistiche complesse, con particolare attenzione agli impatti economici e regolatori.
* Predisporre reportistica di monitoraggio KPI e analisi di performance, contribuendo all’implementazione di azioni di miglioramento continuo.
* Interagire proattivamente con dipartimenti interni ( IT, Operations, Regulatory ) e stakeholder esterni (prevalentemente fornitori IT), assicurando il rispetto delle segnalazioni effettuate e degli obblighi normativi.


**Perchè tu?**


* Laurea o diploma in discipline tecniche
* Esperienze nell’ambito metering/DSO/billing del settore energetico di almeno 2 anni
* Ottima conoscenza dei principali applicativi di Office Automation (es. Office Word, Excel, Power Point)
* Capacità di lavorare in team anche usando strumenti di condivisione
* Capacità di lavorare per scadenze temporali
* Capacità di analisi dati e presentazione degli stessi


Completano il profilo la conoscenza del Sistema Neta, capacità di operare efficacemente in contesti ad alta complessità e caratterizzati da scadenze stringenti, unita a un solido orientamento al problem solving.


*In ENGIE, ogni talento ha un ruolo fondamentale nell’accelerare la transizione energetica.*


*Contribuisci a fare la differenza e vivi un’esperienza professionale ricca di soddisfazioni.*


*Affronta sfide stimolanti e costruisci un percorso di carriera che rispecchi davvero chi sei.*


*Unisciti a noi e partecipa all’avventura del secolo!*

  


**Perché scegliere ENGIE?**


*Lavorare in ENGIE, gruppo multinazionale, significa avere un'ampia varietà di possibilità di carriera in oltre 30 paesi.*


*Ma significa anche mettere la comunità al centro della tua vita professionale e contribuire a un vero cambiamento nel corso di una carriera stimolante.*


*Il nostro impegno è chiaro: sosteniamo un modello sociale che concilia la performance economica con l'impatto positivo sulle persone e sul pianeta. Scommettiamo sul potere della collettività e agiamo come parte di una comunità impegnata a sconvolgere e reinventare il futuro. Diversità e inclusione sono al centro della nostra cultura aziendale.*


*ENGIE si impegna a combattere tutte le forme di discriminazione in materia di occupazione, accesso alla formazione e promozione. Pertanto, l'integrazione e il sostegno di giovani, adulti, persone con disabilità, senza alcuna distinzione di genere, etnia o religione, sono priorità fondamentali della politica delle risorse umane.* *Riconosciuti per il 2025 dal Sole24Ore come azienda leader in diversità \& inclusione, le certificazioni ISO 30415:2021 e UNI PDR 125:2022 sono l’ulteriore dimostrazione del nostro impegno.*

title: Tech Lead - Mobile Platform (Remote, Full-Time) [AS227] Europe
description:
**About Smart Working**



At Smart Working, we believe your job should not only look right on paper but also feel right every day. This isn’t just another remote opportunity \- it’s about finding where you truly belong, no matter where you are. From day one, you’re welcomed into a genuine community that values your growth and well\-being.



Our mission is simple: to break down geographic barriers and connect skilled professionals with outstanding global teams and products for full\-time, long\-term roles. We help you discover meaningful work with teams that invest in your success, where you’re empowered to grow personally and professionally.  

Join one of the highest\-rated workplaces on Glassdoor and experience what it means to thrive in a truly remote\-first world.


**About the Role**



We are looking for a **Tech Lead \- Mobile Platform**to own and strengthen the technical foundations of our mobile banking app. This is a platform\-focused leadership role, not a product feature lead or purely hands\-on mobile IC position, with a strong emphasis on platform enablement, developer experience, engineering architecture, release quality, and mobile standards. The role supports a small mobile team of approximately 3 engineers working primarily with React Native across iOS and Android.

  

  

The primary purpose of this role is to ensure that the mobile platform operates as a reliable, high\-quality foundation so that product teams can move faster with confidence. This means understanding the product teams' needs, removing blockers, driving adoption of new tools and processes through mentoring, documentation and training, and setting high engineering standards for the mobile codebase, while carefully managing the trade\-off between standards and product team flow.  

  

While the role is primarily leadership\-focused, it requires sufficient hands\-on depth to guide architectural decisions and platform improvements. The team currently has native libraries written in Kotlin, Swift, Objective\-C, and Java that require ongoing maintenance and support. There is also a future consideration around a potential move to Expo, which would be explored and decided collaboratively with the new hire.


### **Responsibilities**

* Lead, mentor, and provide technical direction to the mobile platform team, with a focus on enabling product teams to operate as efficiently as possible through clear prioritisation of platform initiatives.
* Define, implement, and uphold engineering standards across mobile security, observability, performance, resilience, and release quality, continuously balancing standards improvement with minimal impact on product team velocity.
* Act as a hands\-on technical leader in the development and maintenance of shared mobile platform capabilities within React Native and TypeScript.
* Contribute to architectural decisions relating to React Native, native modules, and integrations across Kotlin, Swift, Objective\-C, and Java.
* Own and drive improvements to CI/CD pipelines, build reliability, release workflows, and deployment processes, a critical and essential responsibility for this role.
* Own mobile platform\-level observability and crash reporting, including tooling for logging, analytics, exception monitoring, and production stability; general service alerting is managed by product domain teams.
* Partner with product feature teams to ensure releases are delivered smoothly, meet quality expectations, and align with regulatory and compliance requirements.
* Drive improvements in automated testing practices, including unit, integration, and end\-to\-end testing approaches that improve confidence in release quality.
* Own and coordinate production incident management and release activities, ensuring timely resolution, clear communication, and strong operational control.
* Collaborate with design and product teams to evolve and maintain shared UI components, platform standards, and mobile design system capabilities.
* Drive adoption of new tools, processes, and standards across product teams through mentoring, documentation, and training, ensuring improvements are embedded in practice, not just defined on paper.
* Identify and drive opportunities to improve platform stability, developer productivity, and the long\-term maintainability of the mobile application estate.
* Promote secure application development practices and support compliance\-aligned delivery within a regulated banking environment.
* Evaluate tooling evolution where relevant, including potential future considerations such as a move to Expo, in close collaboration with the team.

### **Requirements**

* 8\+ years of experience in mobile engineering, including significant time operating at a Tech Lead, Mobile Platform Lead, Staff, or Principal Engineer level.
* 4\+ years of production experience building and maintaining mobile applications with React Native and TypeScript, this is the core of the stack.
* 4\+ years of experience with native mobile development (Kotlin, Swift, Objective\-C, or Java), required for maintaining and supporting native libraries already in use.
* Own and drive improvements to CI/CD pipelines, build reliability, release processes, deployment workflows, and broader developer workflows, this is an essential requirement for the role.
* Strong understanding of mobile architecture, including React Native bridges, native modules, and platform\-level architectural decisions.
* Experience owning or significantly improving mobile platform observability, crash reporting (e.g. Firebase Crashlytics, Sentry, Datadog), exception monitoring, and production incident handling.
* Strong experience with mobile testing approaches, including unit, integration, and end\-to\-end testing (e.g. Detox, Appium, Jest) to drive release confidence.
* Solid understanding of mobile engineering standards across performance, resilience, reliability, and quality.
* 1\+ year of experience leading a mobile platform team, with demonstrated responsibility for technical direction, mentoring, and engineering standards.
* Track record of understanding product team needs and prioritising platform work to remove blockers and accelerate product delivery.
* Demonstrated ability to drive adoption of new tools and processes across teams, through documentation, training, and hands\-on mentoring, not just defining standards.
* Experience setting and maintaining a high\-quality bar for a mobile codebase while managing the ongoing trade\-off between standards and team velocity.
* Strong cross\-functional collaboration skills across engineering, product, design, and where relevant, compliance and regulatory stakeholders.
* Prior experience leading at least one significant technical or platform project end\-to\-end.
* Experience managing or directly leading engineers, with a people\-first approach to technical leadership.

### **Nice to Have**

* Prior experience in a fintech, banking, or regulated product environment
* Stronger depth in native mobile development (Kotlin, Swift, Objective\-C, or Java) beyond integration and maintenance support.
* Experience evaluating or supporting React Native tooling evolution, including Expo or similar migration considerations.
* Exposure to shared platform components, design systems, or reusable mobile UI library development and maintenance.
* Experience with secure mobile development practices or a background that includes mobile security\-focused work.
* Familiarity with mobile performance optimisation, resilience patterns, or broader non\-functional quality standards.

title: Managing Director - MRS-GM Italy (m/f/d*)
description:
**Location** Milan, Italy **Job type** Full\-Time **Work mode** Hybrid **Job level** Management \& Leadership **Job ID** 12519 **Company** Munich Re Specialty \- Global Markets **Employment type** Regular **Area of expertise** Client \& Customer Management
  
**About Us**
At Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets (MRS\-GM), it is our ambition to become the leading Primary Specialty Insurance provider, underpinned by an effective and adaptable strategy, superior products, and industry leaders working in a supportive environment to achieve this.
At the heart of our success is a strong culture where people are encouraged to be present, bold and curious, allowing them to achieve their individual goals.*Please see our website for more information.***Managing Director, MRS\-GM Italy**
Munich Re Specialty was formed as a stand\-alone division in 2023, bringing together the wider Munich Re Group’s primary specialty insurance businesses. Building on strong foundations in the UK and the US, **Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets (MRS****‑****GM)** is executing an ambitious strategy to expand its footprint across key European markets.
As part of this growth journey, we are seeking a Managing Director, MRS‑GM Italy, to establish, lead, and grow our presence in the Italian market.
This is a senior, market‑facing leadership role with full accountability for defining and executing the Italian business strategy, building local underwriting and distribution capabilities, and establishing Munich Re Specialty’s Italian operations from the ground up. As **Managing Director**, you will define and execute the local strategy, build a high‑performing team, and act as the face and voice of Munich Re Specialty in Italy.
Working in close collaboration with senior leaders across Europe and the wider Munich Re Group, you will combine entrepreneurial drive with disciplined underwriting and operational excellence to deliver sustainable and profitable growth, while upholding Munich Re’s reputation for integrity and quality.
This role is ideally suited to an influential, market‑facing leader who enjoys building businesses, developing teams, and shaping strategy within a global specialty insurance environment.
The role reports to the CEO, MRS‑GM Europe.**Responsibilities****Strategic \& Business Leadership*** Create and execute a comprehensive, scalable, and sustainable strategy for the Italian market, aligned with MRS‑GM’s European and global objectives.
* Establish Munich Re Specialty’s presence in Italy, growing the business to EUR 100m\+ within five years with a focus on the middle market.
* Implement an efficient and scalable operating model that supports long‑term growth and future European expansion.
* Act as the face and voice of MRS‑GM in Italy, embodying and representing the Munich Re Specialty brand in the market.

**Market Development \& Client Engagement*** Develop and maintain long‑term relationships with global, regional, and local brokers, MGAs, and key clients to drive new business and strengthen existing partnerships.
* Actively monitor market developments, competitor activity, and emerging opportunities to enhance MRS‑GM’s market positioning.
* Proactively engage with internal stakeholders locally and internationally (including London and Munich), as well as Reinsurance, Facultative \& Corporate and Great Lakes, to ensure underwriting and business best practices are applied.

**Leadership \& People Development*** Recruit, develop, and lead a local Italian team from the ground up (‘boots on the ground’), starting with underwriting capabilities.
* Set clear objectives and support staff development through continuous performance conversations, coaching, and knowledge sharing.
* Ensure that all team members meet operational, regulatory, and compliance requirements.

**Governance, Performance \& Risk Management*** Ensure that underwriting, distribution, and operational activities align with the Italian business plan and global underwriting strategy.
* Oversee portfolio performance, ensuring disciplined underwriting, regulatory compliance, and sustainable profitability, working in close partnership with Great Lakes Italy to support adherence to governance and regulatory standards.
* Conduct peer reviews and ensure adherence to delegated authorities, company procedures, and compliance standards.
* Produce management reports and insights as required to support decision‑making and performance tracking.
* Ensure adherence to delegated authorities, company procedures, and compliance standards through peer review and disciplined oversight.

**Knowledge and Skills*** Extensive, proven experience as a senior business leader within the primary insurance market in Italy, with a strong track record of delivering profitable growth.
* Highly regarded and well‑connected within the Italian insurance market, with an established portfolio of senior broker relationships.
* Strong understanding of operating within a regulated financial services environment, with the ability to act as an approved senior person in the role.
* Entrepreneurial, resilient, and comfortable building operations in an evolving or greenfield setup.
* Strategic thinker with the ability to articulate and execute ambitious business plans while inspiring teams to deliver results.
* Strong commercial judgment, solution‑oriented mindset, and ability to balance innovation with governance.
* Credibility at senior stakeholder level, underpinned by integrity, professionalism, and sound decision‑making.
* International mindset with strong people leadership, change management, and emotional intelligence.
* Fluent in Italian and English, written and spoken.

*If you are motivated by the opportunity to establish and lead Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets in Italy, we would be delighted to hear from you.***Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion**
At Munich Re, Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion foster innovation and resilience and enable us to act braver and better. Embracing the power of DEI is at the core of who we are. We recognise diversity can be multi\-dimensional, intersectional, and complex, so we want to build a diverse workforce that includes a wide range of racial, ethnic, sexual, and gender identities; economic and geographic backgrounds; physical abilities; ages; life, school, and career experiences; and political, religious, and personal beliefs. Additionally, we are committed to building an equitable and inclusive work environment where this diversity is celebrated, valued, and has equitable opportunities to succeed.
All candidates in consideration for any role can request a reasonable adjustment at any point in our recruitment process. You can request an adjustment by speaking to your Talent Acquisition contact.**Learning and innovating today, striving for sustainable societies and business tomorrow**
At Munich Re Specialty – Global Markets our approach to ESG is underpinned by our desire to seize business opportunities and to nurture a stimulating and inclusive work environment. Our ESG strategy aims to deliver holistic impacts across environmental, social and governance topics including supporting a number of local initiatives within our community and offering volunteering opportunities for colleagues
Learn more about sustainability at Munich Re – choose your impact!**\#BePresent** **\#BeBold** **\#BeCurious**
  
**Your Benefits**
-----------------


With us, you get more than just an exciting job. Enjoy a wide range of employee benefits tailored to your wellbeing and development. Please note that regional differences may apply.


Competitive Salary


We provide fair and competitive compensation that reflects your performance and commitment.


Company performance\-based Incentives

Recognition and Special Rewards

Health \& Wellbeing

title: Legal Counsel (Italy & Nordics)
description:
**THE GOAL**


The company is looking for a full\-time in\-house lawyer that can be based in London or Milan. The purpose of the role is to provide commercial and pragmatic legal advice for an international FMCG, and to help the business to achieve the company goals, among corporate projects.


In a very fast paced environment, you will be encouraged to take on a great range of responsibilities.

**WORKING RELATIONSHIPS**


Department: Legal Team


Reporting to: General Counsel


Working with (external and internal relationships): Legal Team, Italy and Nordics team

**WHY YOU SHOULD JOIN US**

* For the opportunity to get to work with an international community and provide support to multiple jurisdictions
* For the opportunity to be autonomous at the same time as being surrounded by a tight supportive team that works collaboratively
* For the amazing culture with a supportive leadership team and smart teammates who want to make a genuine difference
* For the opportunity to work with people who you can learn from and grow

**WHAT YOU DO**


Main responsibilities:

* Act as first point of contact for Italy and Nordics legal matters.
* Draft and negotiate commercial contracts including but not limited to retailer agreements, franchise agreements, service agreements, supply and distributor contracts and confidentiality agreements.
* Provide advice to commercial teams on contract interpretation.
* Support clients in negotiations with suppliers and internal stakeholders.
* Draft legal correspondence, including warning letters, termination notices, and other documents related to franchise operations.
* Act as the primary legal and compliance advisor for Italy and the Nordics (Sweden and Finland), ensuring alignment with local regulatory requirements.
* General office administration duties, including managing contract signings and filing on the shared drive.
* Oversee corporate governance requirements, including board minutes, statutory filings, and regulatory documentation for Italy and the Nordics (Sweden and Finland).
* Conduct legal research using Practical Law, LexisNexis, and be comfortable leveraging GenAI tools available within the organisation.
* Create and maintain relationships with external lawyers in Italy and the Nordics (Sweden and Finland).


Environment:

* Legal Team of 6 people working across 12 jurisdictions

**WHO YOU ARE**

* You have at least 6\-year PQE
* You are qualified as a solicitor in Italy with a minimum of 4 years’ experience as an in\-house lawyer (experience in FMCG is a plus)
* You are fluent in Italian and English; familiarity with Swedish or Finnish would be beneficial but is not essential
* You are a strong generalist lawyer with legal experience aligned to the goals set out above, including commercial contracting, regulatory compliance, and corporate governance
* You have strong experience drafting, reviewing, and negotiating commercial contracts
* You are comfortable navigating regulatory frameworks relevant to food, retail, and consumer\-facing businesses
* You have strong communications skills and the ability to build effective partnerships with multiple stakeholders across different countries
* You are comfortable using legal research tools and GenAI enabled platforms to support efficient, high\-quality legal work
* You exercise sound judgment and can balance requirements with strategic and commercial objectives
* You thrive in a fast\-paced, multicultural environment and are a team player with a proactive, business\-oriented mindset

**ABOUT US**  

KellyDeli, founded in 2010, is an innovative and rapidly expanding food retail and restaurant company. Sushi Daily, one of its brand concepts, is the leading European provider of fully\-serviced sushi bars, currently operating over 1,000 kiosks across 13 countries.


Our unique concept features outlets in grocery stores, supermarkets, and airports, where live Sushi Artisans create their art in front of customers, providing a unique customer experience and top\-quality sushi.

**Our Purpose**

*To help the world east better, flourish together \& dream bigger*

 **VALUES**

* Totally Together
* Humbly Honest
* Positively Passionate
* Constantly Curious
* Expertly Excellent
* Adopting Growth Mindset

**BENEFITS**

Sushi Allowance


Wellbeing Allowance


Birthday off


26 days annual leave (excluding national holidays)


Late starts \& early finish X6 of each


Health Insurance


Hybrid working 2/3 split

title: Director, Lurisia Export
description:
The Director Export – Lurisia IT leads the full international expansion, commercial execution, and operational rigor of the Lurisia business across priority global markets. The Lurisia business covers a premium portfolio which is renowned for its natural mineral water sourced from the Western Alps and high\-quality soft drinks made with authentic ingredients. Known for purity, the range includes sparkling lemonades (Limonata), Chinotto, and Aranciata Rossa, often using IGP\-certified fruits for an authentic Mediterranean taste, This senior role is accountable for delivering sustainable volume, NSR, and EBIT growth through strong export operations, distributor leadership, regulatory compliance, and crossfunctional coordination.


The role also acts as the central orchestrator between commercial, supply chain and external partners, ensuring best in class customer experience and flawless execution in\-market.


What You’ll Do for Us

**1\. Export Strategy \& Market Expansion**

* Build and execute the strategic export business for Lurisia across priority geographies, ensuring alignment with growth ambitions and category dynamics.
* Perform opportunity sizing, business case development, and prioritization of new markets and channels.
* Drive visibility and brand positioning in international B2B environments, including participation in key trade shows for At home/AFH prospecting.
**2\. Commercial Leadership \& Distributor Management**

* Lead relationships with distributors, importers and bottlers across top markets, ensuring performance against volume, NSR, and profit targets.
* Oversee end to end customer management, including commercial negotiations, annual contracts, complaints resolution, and ongoing queries.
* Travel regularly to key markets to meet distributors, review performance, and drive in market execution.
**3\. Operational Execution \& Inventory Management**

* Oversee sales stock management, including the lease with CCH and the management of offsite KO lease properties used for export storage.
* Ensure inventory accuracy and tight control of stock movements, availability, and ageing risk.
* Collaborate with Supply Chain to ensure optimized export flows, logistics costs, and ontime fulfilment.
**4\. Production Partner Oversight**

* Act as the main point of contact for all production partners, including CCH
* Ensure production planning, quality standards, documentation, and shipment timelines are met.
* Resolve operational issues and maintain high alignment on innovation
**5\. Order\-to\-Cash \& Commercial Operations**

* Oversee the order generation, validation, and payment cycle.
* Manage post sales customer service, including product recalls, claims, and payment issues, ensuring rapid and transparent resolution.
* Lead clear and timely price communications to partners, including price increases, promotions, and discount policies.
**6\. Cross****Functional Leadership**

* Partner with Marketing to ensure global brand consistency, asset localization, and innovation strategy alignment.
* Work closely with Finance on strong forecast cadence, P\&L ownership, and investment decision\-making.
* Ensure full compliance with legal, regulatory, customs, and product standards across every export market.
 **Experience \& Capabilities**

* 10\+ years of progressive commercial experience in FMCG/CPG, with a minimum of 5 years in export or international markets.
* Proven track record leading P\&Ldriven business units or export territories.
* Strong knowledge of commercial policies, international distribution models, customs processes, and regulatory frameworks.
* Key Account management, particularly in international markets and with wholesalers.
* Demonstrated ability to manage complex partnerships and negotiate senior level agreements.
* Fluency in English and Italian; additional languages are an advantage.
  

Travel  

Up to 40%
